title: ollama/ollama
summary: Get up and running with Llama 3.2, Mistral, Gemma 2, and other large language models locally.

title: ollama-python
summary: Official Python client for Ollama, published on PyPI as `ollama`.

title: ollama-js
summary: Official JavaScript/TypeScript client for Ollama, published on npm as `ollama`.

title: langchain-ollama
summary: LangChain integration package (`langchain-ollama`) exposing Ollama as a chat/LLM provider.

title: ollama-webui
summary: Open-source ChatGPT-style web interface for Ollama models (renamed to Open WebUI).

title: ollama-cli
summary: Unofficial interactive CLI for chatting with Ollama models, installable via pip.

title: ollama-copilot
summary: Experimental GitHub Copilot-like code-completion service backed by Ollama.

title: ollama-rag
summary: Minimal RAG template using Ollama embeddings + Chroma vector store.

title: ollama-discord
summary: Discord bot that exposes Ollama models to guild channels.

title: ollama-helm
summary: Community-maintained Helm chart for deploying Ollama in Kubernetes.

title: ollama4j
summary: Java/JVM client library for Ollama, published to Maven Central.

title: ollama-haystack
summary: Haystack integration by deepset, exposing Ollama as a Generator & Embedder.

title: ollama-n8n
summary: n8n community node to call Ollama chat/generate endpoints in workflows.

title: ollama-curl
summary: Collection of copy-paste curl snippets for every Ollama REST endpoint.

title: ollama-grpc
summary: gRPC proxy that wraps Ollama’s REST API for strongly-typed clients.

title: ollama-slack
summary: Slack bot that answers questions using local Ollama models.

title: ollama-django
summary: Django app + REST wrapper for running Ollama models behind an API gateway.

title: ollama-rust
summary: Community Rust crate (`ollama-rs`) providing typed bindings to the Ollama API.

title: ollama-obsidian
summary: Obsidian plugin that lets you query local Ollama models from within notes.

title: ollama-macos-swift
summary: Swift Package for macOS apps to integrate Ollama via native REST calls.
